entry_conditions.value: Initiation at START node moving into N1.

exit_conditions.value: Transition from N4 to END when an additional pass is no longer justified.
exit_conditions.source_text: N4 -> END [additional pass no longer justified]

exit_conditions.value: Transition from N6 to END after one path remains strongest.

required_steps.value: N1: Expands one starting state into multiple possible paths.
required_steps.source_text: N1 expands one starting state into multiple possible paths.

required_steps.value: Parallel branches / alternatives N2A, N2B, N2C executed or evaluated.
required_steps.ambiguity: Whether N2A, N2B, N2C are concurrent paths or mutually exclusive choices is not explicitly defined.

required_steps.value: N3: Adds a new differentiating input.
required_steps.source_text: N3 adds a new differentiating input.

required_steps.value: N4: Re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.
required_steps.source_text: N4 re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.

optional_steps.value: N5: Reopens the graph when separation remains weak.
optional_steps.source_text: N5 -> N2A, N5 -> N2B, N5 -> N2C; N5 reopens the graph when separation remains weak.

optional_steps.value: N6: Closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.
optional_steps.source_text: N6 -> END; N6 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

loops.value: Loop from N4 through N5 back to N2A/N2B/N2C under the condition that split remains low.
loops.source_text:
N4 -> N5 [split remains low]
N5 -> N2A
N5 -> N2B
N5 -> N2C

branches.value: Three-way divergence from N1 to N2A, N2B, and N2C.
branches.source_text:
N1 -> N2A
N1 -> N2B
N1 -> N2C
branches.ambiguity: It is ambiguous whether this represents a parallel fork or a conditional choice.

branches.value: Three-way conditional branch from N4 based on state evaluations: 1) split remains low -> N5, 2) one path remains strongest -> N6, 3) additional pass no longer justified -> END.
branches.source_text:
N4 -> N5 [split remains low]
N4 -> N6 [one path remains strongest]
N4 -> END [additional pass no longer justified]

termination_conditions.value: Termination reached if additional pass is no longer justified from N4.
termination_conditions.source_text: N4 -> END [additional pass no longer justified]

termination_conditions.value: Termination reached via N6 if one path remains strongest after re-sorting.
termination_conditions.source_text:
N4 -> N6 [one path remains strongest]
N6 -> END

structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Divergent-convergent path generation with conditional feedback loop and multiple termination pathways.

primitive_layer.dominant_primitive: Path evaluation and state filtering loop

preconditions.value: Must start from a single initial state to expand.
preconditions.source_text: N1 expands one starting state into multiple possible paths.

postconditions.value: Graph terminates either because one path is strongest or further passes are unjustified.
postconditions.source_text:
N4 -> END [additional pass no longer justified]
N6 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

stopping_criteria.source_text: N4 -> END [additional pass no longer justified]

stopping_criteria.value: One path remains strongest after re-sorting.
stopping_criteria.source_text:
N4 -> N6 [one path remains strongest]
N6 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

validity_conditions.value: Looping requires a low split condition.

body.expression: N1: Expand starting state into paths N2A, N2B, N2C

cases.action: N5: Reopen graph and loop back to N2 paths
N6: Close graph and go to END

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure begins by taking a single starting state and expanding it into three divergent paths (N2A, N2B, N2C). A new differentiating input is introduced at N3, after which N4 re-evaluates and re-sorts the fit of these paths. Depending on the outcome of this re-sorting, the workflow branches: if the split between paths remains low, N5 reopens the graph and loops back to the paths; if one path emerges as the strongest, N6 closes the graph and routes to completion; if an additional pass is deemed unjustified, the procedure terminates directly.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: START -> N1 -> [N2A, N2B, N2C] -> N3 -> N4 -> If split low Then N5 -> [N2A, N2B, N2C] Else If strongest path found Then N6 -> END Else If no pass justified Then END

ambiguities.description: Nature of divergence from N1 to N2 sub-nodes.
plausible_interpretations: Parallel execution of all three paths.
Conditional or non-deterministic selection of one path out of the three options.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Affects the structural categorization of the N2 layer as either a parallel block or a choice block.

notes: Extraction based strictly on the provided topological structure and explicit node behavior notes without introducing domain-specific interpretations.
